title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was awarded the 'Cy Yuk' by The Athletic for a 10.35 ERA in eight Red Sox starts.
Bello's performance included 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings, with a 5.3 BB/9 and 5.6 K/9 ratios.
Despite bullpen success, Boston kept Bello in Triple-A instead of recalling him, choosing Eduardo Rivera for rotation needs.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox consider buying or selling as they're less than two games from a wildcard spot.
Boston's recent rebound includes a seven-game streak despite injuries to key players.
The team faces uncertainty as similar indecision at past deadlines hindered long-term strategy.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira's concert at TD Garden marked her return after a seven-year music hiatus with energetic performances.
The show combined nostalgia and new beginnings, featuring songs like 'Hips Don't Lie' and new tracks from 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.
Shakira donned diverse costumes and offered a visual spectacle with elements like a she-wolf inflatable and dance routines.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: $18.6 million in tax credits will fund 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Development projects include converting historic sites into apartments in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
The Housing Development Incentive Program targets economic improvement in mid-sized cities with high growth potential.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested by SWAT in Albany, New York for bank robberies in Nashua, NH, and Boonton, NJ.
Sawyer allegedly used a black semiautomatic handgun during the robberies, stealing $6,000 and $12,000 respectively.
The arrests were part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0 involving FBI and multiple police departments across states.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near his motorcycle off Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, on Thursday night.
Grondin, a Berlin resident, had been missing since July 4; he was last seen riding a Harley-Davidson motorcycle.
Preliminary investigation shows Grondin crossed into the eastbound lane, hit a post, and crashed into the woods.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Governor Maura Healey declared July 11 as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts to honor the singer-songwriter.
Noah Kahan became the first artist to headline four sold-out concerts at Fenway Park, attracting over 150,000 fans.
Kahan is praised for his music reflecting New England's culture and his advocacy for mental health awareness.

title: ‘Let’s just call it a tour’: Judy Collins says her farewell tour isn’t a farewell
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Judy Collins, 87, clarifies her 'farewell tour' is not an actual farewell, likening it to other artists' multiple farewell tours.
The 'Sweet Judy Blue Eyes' tour will feature performances in Cape Cod, Cohasset, Bar Harbor, and Tanglewood, ending in March 2027.
The tour will be filmed for a documentary on Collins's life and legacy, showcasing her continuous commitment to touring and performing.

title: 3 new movies to watch on Netflix in July
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'A League of Their Own', 'Moneyball', and 'Gone Girl' to its streaming library in July.
'A League of Their Own' features Geena Davis and Tom Hanks in a story about a 1940s women's baseball team.
'Moneyball' stars Brad Pitt and Jonah Hill, focusing on Oakland Athletics GM Billy Beane's innovative strategies.

title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox won their seventh straight game, defeating the New York Mets 6-2, improving their record to 44-48.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run, helping Boston stay 1.5 games back from the AL's third wild-card spot.
Anthony Seigler hit a crucial two-run homer during travel delays, contributing to the team's ongoing offensive success.
